en_title: PlayStation’s Physical Media-Free Future Isn’t Just Concerning, It’s Offensive
cn_title: PlayStation的无实体未来不只是令人担忧，而是在冒犯玩家
subtitle: 买了也会被收走
opus_summary: 索尼刚通知用户将从PSN移除550多部已购电影，又确认2028年后PlayStation内容全面转向数字发行。作者认为这证明数字购买只是可撤销许可，也会伤害实体收藏、向后兼容与游戏保存。

paragraphs.en: PlayStation has suddenly told its audience to brace themselves for an entirely physical media-free future. The news comes just days after the company unapologetically informed users it was about to permanently delete more than 550 Studio Canal-distributed movies from the PlayStation Network – and literally remove them from the accounts of any customer who previously purchased them.
paragraphs.cn: PlayStation突然告诉自己的用户，要准备迎接一个完全没有实体媒介的未来。就在几天前，这家公司才毫无歉意地通知用户，它将从PlayStation Network永久删除550多部由Studio Canal发行的电影，并且会把这些电影从此前购买过它们的用户账户中真正移除。

paragraphs.en: It’s honestly one of the baffling corporate failures to read the room since Microsoft handed Don Mattrick a microphone and allowed him to discuss how the Xbox One probably wasn’t going to suit someone serving on a nuclear submarine . That is, PlayStation is following up a brazenly anti-consumer rugpull of digital content with fresh confirmation that ALL PlayStation content from 2028 onwards will be exclusively digital.
paragraphs.cn: 老实说，这是自微软把麦克风交给Don Mattrick、让他去解释XBOX One可能不适合在核潜艇上服役的人以来，最让人看不懂场合的企业失误之一。换句话说，PlayStation刚刚对数字内容来了一次明目张胆的反消费者抽梯子，现在又确认2028年之后所有PlayStation内容都将只提供数字版。

paragraphs.en: The timing would be hilarious if it wasn’t so appallingly offensive.
paragraphs.cn: 如果不是冒犯到令人震惊，这个时机本来会很好笑。

paragraphs.en: However, the facts are in, and the standard has been set: you cannot trust digital purchases, and you cannot trust the corporations that offer them.
paragraphs.cn: 但事实已经摆在这里，标准也已经立下了：你不能信任数字购买，也不能信任提供这些购买的公司。

paragraphs.en: While I maintain a library of physical games I love – and I regularly browse charity stores and my local CeX for hard copies of things I know I’ll enjoy having stashed on a shelf – allow me to qualify that I have indeed purchased and downloaded many digital games over the decades, via console and PC storefronts. Provided they always remain available to redownload – even if delisted from sale – it’s a compromise I’ve been willing to concede, for now. Credit where credit’s due, it’s been a pretty workable arrangement to date. For instance, when the disc tray on my Xbox 360 turned to dust during the production of a recent racing game retrospective I put together to celebrate IGN’s 30th anniversary, I found myself pulling down 20-year-old, long-delisted DLC on a replacement console without much fuss.
paragraphs.cn: 我自己保有一批喜爱的实体游戏，也经常去慈善商店和本地CeX淘那些我知道会乐意摆上架子的实体盘。不过也要说明，过去几十年里，我确实通过主机和PC商店购买并下载过很多数字游戏。只要它们始终可以重新下载，哪怕已经从商店下架，这暂时还是我愿意接受的一种妥协。该给的肯定还是要给，到目前为止，这套安排还算能运转。比如，我最近为了庆祝IGN三十周年做一篇赛车游戏回顾时，XBOX 360的光驱托盘彻底报废了，但我还是能在替换主机上相当顺利地下载20年前、早已下架的DLC。

paragraphs.en: Of course, as much as I appreciate this functionality, I’m not going to celebrate it. The ability to simply re-download content we’ve purchased in the past should be a bare-minimum service. I’m not asking for content to be hand delivered to me; I’m asking for a server to be left switched on. If non-profits like the Internet Archive can do it, I’m sure commercial video game juggernauts can manage likewise.
paragraphs.cn: 当然，尽管我感谢这种功能，但我不会为它欢呼。能够重新下载我们过去购买过的内容，本来就应该是最低限度的服务。我不是要求有人把内容亲手送到我门口，我只是要求服务器继续开着。如果互联网档案馆这样的非营利组织都能做到，我相信商业电子游戏巨头也能做到。

paragraphs.en: On the flip side, I have never, ever purchased a movie digitally. My household still subscribes to several streaming services, and I’m content enough with their convenience for the time being, but my movie collection is otherwise strictly physical. Prior to entering the media industry I worked at Blockbuster Video during the transition from VHS to DVD, which is where my collection started. I’m hesitant to put a figure on its current size on the off chance that my wife reads this, but I will admit I’m… deeply nostalgic about video stores. Like, proper deeply. If I can’t visit them anymore, building one around me was the next best thing. Now I can work at IGN while still ensconced in a video store. It’s the best of both worlds.
paragraphs.cn: 反过来说，我从来没有、真的从来没有以数字形式购买过电影。我们家现在仍然订阅几个流媒体服务，我暂时也还算满意它们的便利性，但我的电影收藏除此之外严格坚持实体。在进入媒体行业之前，我曾在Blockbuster Video工作，正赶上VHS转向DVD的时期，我的收藏也是从那时开始的。我不太想给出现在的规模，免得我妻子看到，但我承认自己对录像带租赁店有着非常深的怀旧情结。真的很深。既然现在不能再去这样的店，那在自己身边搭出一家就是次优选择。现在我能一边在IGN工作，一边仍然被一家录像店包围。这就是两全其美。

paragraphs.en: At any rate, I’ve never had an interest in starting a digital library of movies beholden to any one vendor. I’ve just never trusted the concept. Frankly, the Studio Canal debacle on PlayStation Network is all the reason I need to ensure I never will.
paragraphs.cn: 无论如何，我从来没兴趣建立一个受制于某一家供应商的数字电影库。我一直不信任这个概念。坦白说，PlayStation Network这次Studio Canal事件，已经足以让我确信自己永远不会这么做。

paragraphs.en: Sony discontinued the ability to make movie and TV purchases and rentals on the PlayStation Store back in August 2021. However, users were still able to watch the content they’d purchased beyond that cut-off. However, it appears those purchases were always on a ticking clock. Whatever agreement Sony and Studio Canal had for that content to remain hosted on PSN for people who bought it has either lapsed or completely imploded. We can’t know, because neither company has explained or apologised for it. IGN has sought comment. At the time of writing it hasn’t been forthcoming.
paragraphs.cn: 索尼早在2021年8月就停止了PlayStation Store上的电影和电视购买与租赁功能。不过，用户在那个截止日期之后仍然可以观看自己已经购买的内容。现在看来，那些购买从一开始就带着倒计时。无论索尼和Studio Canal之间关于这些内容继续托管在PSN、供购买者观看的协议是到期了，还是彻底崩了，我们都无从得知，因为两家公司都没有解释，也没有道歉。IGN已经寻求评论。截至发稿时，尚未收到回复。

paragraphs.en: For the corporate bootlickers in the back, we’ve read the legalese. You don’t need to parrot it back. We’re all aware of the insidious terms and conditions that companies apply to transactions for digital media in order to be able to snatch it away without consequence. We’re aware of the fact that, despite companies being clearly permitted to use words like “buy” and “purchase”, we’re only ever really leasing a license to access content – a license companies can revoke at their discretion.
paragraphs.cn: 给后排那些替公司说话的人听清楚：法律条款我们读过了，不用你们复读。我们都知道，公司会把阴险的条款和条件加在数字媒体交易上，好让它们可以在不承担后果的情况下把内容夺走。我们也知道，尽管公司显然被允许使用「购买」这样的字眼，但我们实际得到的永远只是访问内容的一份许可，而这份许可可以由公司自行撤销。

paragraphs.en: The fact remains is this: if someone entered my home and scooped up my copies of Terminator 2, This Is Spinal Tap, Total Recall, Cliffhanger, Highlander, Leon, First Blood, and whatever else they saw fit to steal – that’s theft. That it happens with a line of code being rewritten or deleted doesn’t make it less so.
paragraphs.cn: 事实仍然是这样：如果有人闯进我家，把我的《终结者2》《摇滚万万岁》《全面回忆》《绝岭雄风》《高地人》《这个杀手不太冷》《第一滴血》，以及他们认为合适的任何东西一扫而空，那就是盗窃。哪怕这件事是通过重写或删除一行代码完成的，也不会让它变得不那么像盗窃。

paragraphs.en: So what exactly does an exclusively digital future really look like?
paragraphs.cn: 那么，一个完全数字化的未来到底是什么样子？

paragraphs.en: I’ve already had an appetiser of it. In 2024, Ubisoft announced The Crew – a full-price, premium game with multiple pieces of paid DLC in the form of car packs and major expansions – was to be nuked from existence and removed from the libraries of customers who bought it . Ubisoft was hit with a lawsuit over it, which it responded to by insisting that buying a game only gives players a “limited license to access the game.”
paragraphs.cn: 我已经尝过开胃菜了。2024年，育碧宣布《飙酷车神》这款全价高级游戏将被从存在中抹除，并从购买它的用户库中移除。这款游戏还包含多项付费DLC，包括车辆包和大型扩展内容。育碧因此遭到起诉，而它的回应是坚称购买游戏只会给玩家一份「访问游戏的有限许可」。

paragraphs.en: The explanation went down like a lead balloon.
paragraphs.cn: 这个解释砸得像一块铅气球。

paragraphs.en: It’s an imperfect example, since not even owning The Crew on disc helps in this particular instance. Despite having full support for single-player progress, it’s an online-only game, and the discs are now worthless. It was, nonetheless, an effective glimpse at a future where games can be yanked away, leaving consumers conned out of their cash.
paragraphs.cn: 这个例子并不完美，因为在这个特定案例里，即便拥有《飙酷车神》的实体盘也没用。尽管游戏完全支持单人进度，但它是一款全程联网游戏，那些光盘如今已经毫无价值。不过，它仍然有效地让人看见了一个未来：游戏可以被直接抽走，消费者则像被骗走了钱一样被留在原地。

paragraphs.en: Consumers gain nothing of worth from an all-digital future.
paragraphs.cn: 消费者无法从全数字未来中得到任何有价值的东西。

paragraphs.en: We’re now staring down the barrel of a future where the PS6 doesn’t come with a disc drive . Separate reports are indicating that Xbox may also be moving away from physical media, and that the next-generation Project Helix device will also not feature a disc drive. It’s a philosophy that will likely sideline anyone with a curated collection of physical games, or even a hybrid library that’s part disc, part digital. So it’s a rake in the face for backwards compatibility, and an entirely unappealing future for vintage gamers – the support of whom laid the bedrock for the business today.
paragraphs.cn: 我们现在正直面一个PS6不配备光驱的未来。另有报道显示，XBOX也可能正在远离实体媒介，下一代Project Helix设备同样不会配备光驱。这种理念很可能会把那些精心收藏实体游戏的人，甚至是实体盘和数字版混合收藏的人，全部推到边缘。所以，这等于给向后兼容迎面来了一耙，也给复古玩家描绘了一个毫无吸引力的未来，而正是这些玩家的支持为今天的业务奠定了基础。

paragraphs.en: What impact does an all-digital future have on certain genres of games? An uncertain one, at best. No one is denying the seismic shift in consumer preference, or how digital game sales do indeed eclipse physical sales, broadly. However, as noted in a 2025 analysis from The Game Business , there are certain genres of games that still sell well on disc. At least across the UK, single-player, story-driven games and family games have regularly seen much stronger physical sales than typical. That is, almost half of Hogwart’s Legacy, Assassin’s Creed Mirage, and Resident Evil 4 Remake sales were physical. In the case of Spider-Man 2 and Astro Bot, the figure was reportedly over half. How long will publishers continue to invest in the sorts of single-player games many people still crave if it becomes apparent the audience isn’t fully migrating?
paragraphs.cn: 全数字未来会对某些游戏类型造成什么影响？最多也只能说是不确定。没人否认消费者偏好的巨大转变，也没人否认整体上数字游戏销量确实超过了实体销量。不过，正如The Game Business在2025年的一篇分析所指出的，某些类型的游戏仍然在实体盘上卖得不错。至少在英国，单人叙事游戏和家庭游戏的实体销量经常明显强于一般水平。也就是说，《霍格沃茨之遗》《刺客信条：幻景》和《生化危机4重制版》几乎有一半销量来自实体版。据报道，《漫威蜘蛛侠2》和《宇宙机器人》的这一比例还超过了一半。如果事实证明受众并没有完全迁移，发行商还会继续投资许多人仍然渴望的那类单人游戏多久？

paragraphs.en: What does it mean for game preservation? Nothing good. Archivists, historians, and museums have been fighting a frustrating battle with the games industry for years, and removing physical games from PlayStation’s future is not going to help in the slightest. Certainly not when trade groups like the US Entertainment Software Association are so demonstrably against the concept of game preservation that it actively maintains the process of restoring old games is illegal “hacking”, and lobbies against academic institutions being permitted to take measures to preserve old games and keep them playable for future generations.
paragraphs.cn: 这对游戏保存意味着什么？没有任何好事。档案工作者、历史学者和博物馆多年来一直在和游戏行业打一场令人沮丧的仗，而把实体游戏从PlayStation未来中移除，丝毫不会帮上忙。尤其是当美国娱乐软件协会这样的行业组织如此明显地反对游戏保存概念，甚至积极坚持认为修复旧游戏是非法的「黑客行为」，并游说阻止学术机构采取措施保存旧游戏、让未来世代仍能游玩时，更是如此。

paragraphs.en: What does it mean for games with limited lifespans due to licensed characters, cars, or music? It means no discovery after the fact. Right now a kid entering the gaming community today can use second-hand marketplaces to wind back the clock and experience games that are no longer available digitally. Weren’t born when the first Forza Horizon game came out, but adore Forza Horizon 6? Well, you can’t download most of them anymore, but there are still discs out there in the wild for you to collect and keep. The same goes for all kinds of delisted gems, from Driver: San Francisco to Driveclub, or old Bond and Spider-Man games. How does this work a decade into an all-digital future? It doesn’t.
paragraphs.cn: 这对那些因为授权角色、汽车或音乐而寿命有限的游戏意味着什么？意味着事后不会再有发现的机会。今天刚进入游戏社区的孩子，可以通过二手市场把时间往回拨，体验那些已经无法数字购买的游戏。你出生时第一部《极限竞速：地平线》还没发售，但你很喜欢《极限竞速：地平线6》？好吧，其中大多数现在已经不能下载了，但外面仍然有实体盘可供你收集和保留。从《Driver: San Francisco》到《驾驶俱乐部》，再到老版邦德和蜘蛛侠游戏，各种下架佳作也同样如此。全数字未来走过十年后，这套机制还能怎样运转？答案是不能。

paragraphs.en: What does it mean if you gravitate towards older, used games – an entirely understandable approach considering cost-of-living spikes? You can’t. What does it mean if you just want to gift a game to someone and watch them install and play it immediately after unwrapping it? You can’t. And what does it mean for people who want to share games with their friends or family? Well, it’s simple.
paragraphs.cn: 如果你倾向于购买较老的二手游戏，这在生活成本飙升的背景下完全可以理解，那又意味着什么？你不能了。如果你只是想把一款游戏送给别人，看着对方拆开包装后马上安装游玩，又意味着什么？你不能了。至于想和朋友或家人分享游戏的人呢？很简单。

paragraphs.en: And here we were, labouring under the impression PlayStation completely understood that one-step process.
paragraphs.cn: 而我们原本还以为，PlayStation完全理解这个一步到位的过程。

paragraphs.en: Let’s reiterate: PlayStation has announced the discontinuation of physical media immediately after telling its customers it was going to delete 550 movies from the accounts of people that paid for them. It’s either baffling naivety, or colossal arrogance. Neither one of those are strong characteristics; I just don’t know which it is.
paragraphs.cn: 再说一遍：PlayStation是在告诉用户将从付费购买者账户中删除550部电影之后，立刻宣布停止实体媒介的。这要么是令人困惑的天真，要么是巨大的傲慢。两者都不是什么强项，我只是不知道到底是哪一种。

paragraphs.en: I’ll tell you what I do know, though. I know that I’m not excited about a prohibitively expensive game console that won’t play my old physical games, upon which anything I purchase could be removed without a speck of a refund or a hint of regret.
paragraphs.cn: 不过我知道一件事。我知道自己不会为一台价格高得让人却步、不能运行我旧实体游戏的主机感到兴奋，更不会为一个我购买的任何东西都可能在没有一分钱退款、没有一丝悔意的情况下被移除的平台感到兴奋。

paragraphs.en: It sure ain’t much of a sales pitch.
paragraphs.cn: 这实在算不上什么有吸引力的销售话术。

paragraphs.en: Luke is a Senior Editor on the IGN reviews team. You can track him down on Bluesky @mrlukereilly to ask him things about stuff.
paragraphs.cn: Luke是IGN评测团队的高级编辑。你可以在Bluesky上通过@mrlukereilly找到他，问他各种事情。
